text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, small caps up, defensives rotate, energy dumped. IWM gained 0.94%, XLK rose 0.82%, and XLU climbed 1.10% as falling USO (-1.20%) and easing rates via TLT (+0.37%) supported rate‑sensitives and bond proxies. Positioning stayed moderately bullish with VIX at 16.8, SPY put‑call at 0.72, and QQQ skew at +8.4% while Nvidia headlines and oil volatility kept traders hedged into a still‑greedy 60.8 Fear & Greed backdrop.

INDICES — VIX closed at 16.8, down vs 20‑day 17.7 and signaling reduced equity vol. Daily returns ranked IWM +0.94%, DIA +0.57%, SPY +0.20%, QQQ +0.19% with STYLE showing SMALL > LARGE by 0.74pp. The most notable cross‑asset move was USO -1.20% against TLT +0.37%, highlighting duration and oil sensitivity, while SPY and QQQ both remained above their 200‑day MAs so no CTA sell trigger fired.

VOLUME — DIA volume ran 1.4x, IWM 1.2x, and RSP 1.2x while SPY traded 0.9x and QQQ was not listed but implied near the 1.01x index average, suggesting attention rotating toward cyclicals and small caps. XLE at 1.2x with price -1.12% showed energy distribution, while XLP at 1.1x with price -1.01% showed defensive selling. XRT at 2.0x with retail strength and XHB at 1.6x with housing strength signaled accumulation in oil‑and‑yield‑sensitive consumption and homebuilders.

SECTORS — XLU Utilities +1.10%, XLK Technology +0.82%, XLV Healthcare +0.69%, XLY Discretionary +0.64%, XLB Materials +0.60%, XLRE Real Estate +0.16%, XLF Financials +0.14%, XLC Comms +0.00%, XLI Industrials -0.12%, XLP Staples -1.01%, XLE Energy -1.12%. Top industries were WGMI +7.45% and DRAM +5.49%, while XES -1.62% and XOP -2.34% anchored the downside with energy‑beta weakness.

THE 7s — Top baskets were Cloud +2.20%, New Staples +2.20%, Fab +2.16%, New Metals +1.86%, Buildout +1.80%, with IBM +12.43% strongest and likely a Cloud leader while SPOT +13.06% led New Staples. Bottom baskets were Medtech -0.52%, Old Staples -1.13%, Energy -1.13%, Software -4.33%, Defense -0.45%, with INTU -20.02% crushing Software and WMT -7.27% dragging Old Staples, showing sharp internal divergence within defensives and software.

FED & LIQUIDITY — FFR stands at 3.50%–3.75% with the next FOMC on Jun 17 and CME probabilities at 100% hold, 0% cut, 0% hike amid a hawk 6, neutral 5, dove 4 split. USD liquidity stress printed 28/100 (Normal) with Reserve Scarcity at the 77th percentile as the most‑stressed component, while TED spread at 0.09% and ON‑RRP at $3.3B signaled benign funding.

POSITIONING — SPY put‑call at 0.72 and QQQ at 1.18 showed index‑level complacency in broad beta but elevated protection in tech. Positive skew in SPY at +4.4% and especially QQQ at +8.4% plus VIX below its 20‑day MA and MOVE at 79.7 above its 74.0 MA pointed to more concern in bonds and mega‑cap tech than in SPX itself. QQQ spot trading above its 725.0 GEX flip suggested dealers still damped intraday swings but could flip if Nvidia‑driven futures weakness deepens.

CROSS-ASSET — USO -1.20% alongside XLE -1.12% and airlines/homebuilders strength showed the classic “oil down, consumers up” transmission. GLD -0.10% and SLV +1.05% alongside UUP flat at 0.00% highlighted a mild precious‑metals divergence likely tied to industrial and AI‑themed silver demand.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 60.8 flagged “Greed” with moderate risk appetite. Biggest single‑name movers were INTU -20.02% (Software), IBM +12.43% (Cloud), and SPOT +13.06% (New Staples), each on 2.9x–8.1x volume. On 20‑day momentum, XLK at +14.60% and Fab at +12.74% led, while XLB at -3.45% and Software at +7.28% but ytd -22.68% lagged, and volume spikes in IBM (accumulation) and INTU (distribution) underscored stock‑picking dispersion around earnings.

WATCH — INTU -20.02% merits focus as a Software 7 constituent with 8.1x volume and potential follow‑through or reversal after guidance disappointment. IBM +12.43% deserves monitoring as a Cloud 7 leader with 3.7x volume and potential sentiment read‑through for AI infra and legacy tech. XLE -1.12% on 1.2x volume needs attention for signs of whether today’s oil‑driven distribution extends or reverts after Iran‑linked crude volatility.
